title: Health-related messages for all tobacco product packaging: Toxicity information - Canada.ca
content:
## Health-related messages for all tobacco product packaging: Toxicity information
- Health Warnings on Packages
- Health Information Messages
- Toxicity Information
- Health Warnings on Products
- Series 1: Health-related messages for all tobacco product packaging Cigarettes Little cigars, cigarette tobacco, and tubes Cigars, pipe tobacco and water pipe tobacco Chewing tobacco and snuff Tobacco products intended for use with devices and other tobacco products
- Little cigars, cigarette tobacco, and tubes
- Cigars, pipe tobacco and water pipe tobacco
- Chewing tobacco and snuff
- Tobacco products intended for use with devices and other tobacco products
- Series 2: Health-related messages for all tobacco product packaging Cigarettes Little cigars, cigarette tobacco, and tubes Cigars, pipe tobacco and water pipe tobacco Chewing tobacco and snuff Tobacco products intended for use with devices and other tobacco products
- Little cigars, cigarette tobacco, and tubes
- Cigars, pipe tobacco and water pipe tobacco
- Chewing tobacco and snuff
- Tobacco products intended for use with devices and other tobacco products
The Tobacco Products Appearance, Packaging and Labelling Regulations (TPAPLR) mandate health-related messages for all tobacco products, as follows:
- Health warnings on packages covering at least 75% of the primary display area of most tobacco product packages
- Health warnings on cigarettes, little cigars with tipping paper, and tubes; health warnings extending from one end of the product on cigarettes without tipping paper
- Health information messages on the inside of cigarettes, little cigars, and cigarette tobacco packages
- Toxicity information on the exterior surface of all packages
The TPAPLR also include a rotation scheme that alternates all health-related messages every 24 to 36 months depending on the product. The health-related messages for each series are provided here as reference.
Note: Health Canada is the copyright owner of all health-related messages except for those marked with an asterisk (*). Reproducing any of these images without prior permission is prohibited. Permission to use the images owned by Health Canada can be requested by submitting this form to pregs@hc-sc.gc.ca . For those images whose copyright is not owned by Health Canada, i.e. those marked with an asterisk (*), please contact pregs@hc-sc.gc.ca to obtain the owner's contact information.

## Series 1: Health-related messages for all tobacco product packaging

### Toxicity Information – Cigarettes
Description – Toxicity Information
There is no safe level of exposure to tobacco smoke.
Description – Toxicity Information
Toxic chemicals in tobacco smoke can cause blood clots and strokes.
Description – Toxicity Information
Puff after puff, you inhale more than 70 chemicals that can cause cancer.
Description – Toxicity Information
There is cadmium in tobacco smoke and in batteries. It can cause lung cancer.
Description – Toxicity Information
Children and non-smokers can get sick and die from exposure to tobacco smoke.
Description – Toxicity Information
There are toxic chemicals in tobacco smoke. They can harm almost every organ in your body.

### Toxicity Information – Little cigars, cigarette tobacco, and tubes
Description – Toxicity Information

Description – Toxicity Information

Description – Toxicity Information

Description – Toxicity Information

Description – Toxicity Information

Description – Toxicity Information

### Toxicity Information – Cigars, pipe tobacco and water pipe tobacco
Description - Toxicity information

Description - Toxicity information
There is cadmium in tobacco smoke and in batteries. It can cause lung cancer.
Description - Toxicity information

### Toxicity Information – Chewing tobacco and snuff
Description - Toxicity Information
This product contains dozens of chemicals that can cause cancer.
Description - Toxicity Information
This product contains nicotine. It is a highly addictive drug.
Description - Toxicity Information
This product contains nitrosamines. These toxic chemicals can cause cancer.
Description - Toxicity Information
This product contains arsenic, nickel, and cadmium. These chemicals can cause cancer.

Description - Toxicity Information
This product contains arsenic, nickel, and cadmium. These chemicals can cause cancer.

## Series 2: Health-related messages for all tobacco product packaging

### Toxicity Information – Cigarettes
Description – Attention
There is hydrogen cyanide in tobacco smoke. It damages the cleaning system of your lungs, allowing harmful chemicals to build up.
Description – Attention
Tobacco smoke can damage your eyes. It can lead to blindness.
Description – Attention
10 drags per smoke x 10 smokes a day. That means harmful chemicals enter your body 36,500 times a year.
Description – Attention
Toxic chemicals in tobacco smoke enter your blood. They can damage your whole body.
Description – Attention
There are toxic chemicals in tobacco smoke. Many can cause cancer.
Description – Attention
Cigarette filters do not keep toxic smoke out of your body.

Description – Attention

### Toxicity Information – Chewing tobacco and snuff
Description – Attention

Description – Attention
Your mouth absorbs the nicotine in this product. It is the drug that causes addiction.
Description – Attention
This product contains toxic chemicals. They can cause cancer in your mouth.
Description – Attention
This product contains cadmium. This toxic chemical is also found in batteries.
